config_equivalence.notes: Grant type and requested scopes matched (password + openid profile email). Access/refresh lifetimes NOT equated (KC realm default 300s vs WSO2 default 3600s). JWT access-token issuance NOT equated (KC default JWT; WSO2 IS 7.0.0 default opaque). Claim-attribute mappings NOT equated (WSO2 ID token lacked email/profile claims despite scopes).

rows.expected: HTTP 200, token_type Bearer, refresh_issued true, id_token JWT contains claim key sub
rows.notes: Both IdPs completed password+openid on itl-lab. Full fixture paths: fixtures/keycloak-token-raw.json, fixtures/wso2-token-raw.json.

rows.expected: Access-token JWT-ness matches only after JWT access-token config is equated; until then do not narrate as product superiority
rows.impact: Migrators that decode access tokens as JWTs will see rich claim_keys_access on Keycloak and an empty set on default WSO2 IS 7.0.0 (opaque token). Equate JWT access-token settings before any implementation_drift claim on access-token claims.
rows.notes: Honest first comparative cell for Case 1. Opaque vs JWT is a lab-config / product-default difference under unmatched equivalence checklist.

rows.expected: expires_in matches only after access-token lifetimes are equated on the checklist; until then do not narrate as product superiority
rows.impact: Clients hardcoding refresh timing or assuming a shared access-token lifetime will behave differently across these IdP defaults (300s vs 3600s). Equate token lifetimes before any implementation_drift claim on expiry semantics.
rows.notes: Numeric expires_in appeared in the normalize table; this row assigns the framework verdict so readers are not left to invent one. Lifetimes were explicitly NOT equated on the Case 1 partial checklist.
